sourceMovie.title: Intimacy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Intimacy works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fifty Shades Freed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Intimacy fans keep landing on Fifty Shades Freed (2018) — the romance is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Fifty Shades Darker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fifty Shades Darker draws from the same romance well as Intimacy, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2017 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Dreamers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Dreamers (2003) captures the romance appeal of Intimacy without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts The Dreamers at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Unfaithful
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance appeal of Intimacy — that bittersweet edge, the drama — translates cleanly into Unfaithful (2002). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Dangerous Liaisons
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Dangerous Liaisons (1988) clearly understood what makes romance work — the same quality that keeps Intimacy rewatchable. Expect the same bittersweet rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Lust, Caution
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Lust, Caution (2007) is the kind of film Intimacy fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The romance overlap is real, but it's the bittersweet emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: My Policeman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: My Policeman shares Intimacy's gift for romance — the 2022 film delivers drama without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Shortbus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Intimacy compelling — the romance, the drama — shows up in Shortbus (2006) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Mona Lisa
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance energy of Intimacy is alive in Mona Lisa (1986), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Focus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Focus (2015) operates in the same romance as Intimacy, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

faq.question: What should I watch after Intimacy?
faq.answer: Try Fifty Shades Freed, Fifty Shades Darker, The Dreamers—each shares DNA with Intimacy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Intimacy?
faq.answer: Intimacy (2001) is best described as Romance, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Intimacy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
